data.title: PNC announces redemption of notes
data.description:
• PNC Financial Services (NYSE:PNC) announced on Tuesday, the redemption of, on Nov. 4, 2024, all outstanding Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes due Dec. 2, 2024 issued by PNC Bank, National Association in the amount of $200M.
  • The securities have an original scheduled maturity date of Dec. 2, 2024. The redemption price will be equal to 100% of the principal amount, plus any accrued and unpaid interest to the redemption date of Nov. 4, 2024.
  • Interest on the Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes will cease to accrue on the redemption date.
  • PNC will also redeem, on Oct. 28, 2024, all outstanding 5.671% Fixed Rate/Floating Rate Senior Notes due Oct. 28, 2025 issued by PNC in the amount of $1B.
  • The securities have an original scheduled maturity date of Oct. 28, 2025.
  • Payment of the redemption price for the Senior Floating Rate Bank Notes and the 5.671% Fixed Rate/Floating Rate Senior Notes will be made through the facilities of the Depository Trust Company.

data.title: CytoSorbents rises as FDA accepts application for device to reduce bleeding risk in bypass surgery
data.description:
• CytoSorbents (NASDAQ:CTSO) announced the U.S. FDA has accepted its de novo application for DrugSorb-ATR, a device to reduce risk of bleeding in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass graft surgery and initiated substantive review.
  • Shares of the stock is up 7% premarket.
  • The FDA De Novo pathway provides a regulatory process for novel medical devices that are found to be low to moderate risk and lack an appropriate predicate device.
  • The company anticipates an FDA decision in 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Martin Midstream buyout opposed by hedge funds that had sought to buy the company
data.description:
Shareholders Nut Tree Capital Management and Caspian Capital said Tuesday the will oppose the takeover of Martin Midstream Partners (NASDAQ:MMLP) by the partnership's largest shareholder, saying the offer significantly undervalues the company and its future prospects.

The hedge funds, which made a competing bid in July to buy Martin Midstream (MMLP) for $4.50/unit, said a takeover by Martin Resource Management would bring "significant upside for itself that rightfully belongs to all MMLP unitholders."

Earlier this month, MRMC agreed to buy the Martin Midstream common units it did not already own for $4.02/unit in cash, raising its initial takeover offer of $3.05/unit.

Nut Tree and Caspian, which own a combined 13.2% of Martin Midstream (MMLP), said they will vote against the MRMC deal and file paperwork with the SEC that will allow them to petition other shareholders to reject the buyout.

However, unitholders allied with MRMC that represent 26% of total common units have said they plan to vote for the deal; the transaction requires the approval of a majority of Martin Midstream's (MMLP) outstanding common units.

data.title: Cognizant Technology slips even as Deutsche Bank ups price target
data.description:
• Cognizant Technology (NASDAQ:CTSH) shares slipped 1% in premarket trading on Tuesday, even as Deutsche Bank upped its price target on the consulting firm.
  • Cognizant is slated to report third-quarter results on Oct. 30 and Deutsche Bank analyst Bryan Keane said he expects the company to earn $1.11 per share on $5.015B in revenue. "Demand for discretionary deals has not appeared to improve as we monitor peer commentary and met with mgmt. at our tech conference in August," Keane wrote. "While discretionary spend should remain weak, CTSH's revamped focus on large deals over the past ~18 months should provide a base for growth while positioning the company to benefit from any rebound in demand."
  • Keane kept his Hold rating but upped his price target to $80 based on peer valuations.

data.title: Cadiz's ATEC Water Systems wins $1.6M in new sales contracts
data.description:
• Cadiz (NASDAQ:CDZI), a water solutions company, said on Tuesday, that its operating subsidiary ATEC Water Systems was awarded three new sales contracts totaling $1.6M for arsenic, iron and manganese filtration systems.
  • The systems will be delivered in 2025.
  • Cadiz (CDZI) shares were up 4.85% in pre-market trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Asset Entities receives extension from Nasdaq to regain compliance
data.description:
• Asset Entities (NASDAQ:ASST) on Tuesday said that it has received an extension until February 17, 2025, to regain compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • Nasdaq granted an extension order on October 17, 2024, after the company recently submitted its plan to regain compliance with listing rules, requiring minimum stockholders’ equity of $2.5M.
  • ASST +6.79% premarket to $1.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan in charts: Copper production, sales drop Y/Y in Q3, gold output slips 14%
Autonomix Medical to conduct a reverse stock split
data.description:
• Autonomix Medical (NASDAQ:AMIX) said it has filed an amendment to its amended and restated certificate of incorporation to effect a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its common stock.
  • The split will take effect on October 24.

data.title: GATX beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY24 outlook
data.description:
• GATX press release (NYSE:GATX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.50 beats by $0.54.
  • Revenue of $405.4M (+12.6% Y/Y) beats by $13.75M.
  • Demand for railcars across GATX's global fleets remains strong; Rail North America’s fleet utilization remains above 99%.
  • Aircraft spare engine portfolio continues strong performance.
  • Investment volume was $504.5 million in the third quarter and totaled over $1.3 billion year to date.
  • Company updates 2024 full-year earnings guidance to $7.50 – 7.70 per diluted share (prior $7.30–$7.70 per diluted share) vs. $7.47 consensus.

data.title: Drugstore dagger: Walmart adds same-day prescription delivery service
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) is adding a new service that has the potential to be disruptive. The retail giant will begin to deliver prescriptions to households in the U.S. in as little as 30 minutes. The service will see customers have the ability to add prescriptions and medication refills to their orders for groceries and other products. Notably, the service will be free for customers of Walmart+

The Bentonville-based company aims to have prescription delivery will be available in 49 states by the end of January, with coverage of more than 86% of U.S. households. Walmart (WM) Chief ecCommerce Office Tom Ward noted that sick shoppers need more than just a prescription. "It's the comfort things such as tea and chicken noodle soup," he told Bloomberg.

In a recent survey, Walmart (WMT) customers were asked how the company could better serve them. Over half (55%) expressed a desire to have their prescriptions delivered along with their groceries and other items they need in a single online order. This service was the most requested by Walmart (WMT) customers who self-identified as time-sensitive, busy families.

The prescription delivery program will put Walmart (WMT) in a more competitive position against Amazon and pharmacy chains such as Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) and CVS Health (CVS). For its part, Amazon (AMZN) is targeting the opening of pharmacies in 20 new cities and offering same-day prescription delivery to nearly half of U.S. households next year. Walgreens (WBA) and CVS Health (CVS) both offer same-day prescription delivery in some select locations. However, even against those retail giants, Walmart (WMT) may be able to leverage its fast delivery times, large network of stores, and wide range of products.
